# Figure description: Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_5.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Страт_колонки_совмещенные_по_скважинам/Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_5.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite stratigraphic/lithological borehole log correlation diagram** (литологический разрез / стратиграфический разрез) — a standard Soviet-era "сводный разрез" (composite section) showing multiple drillhole columns correlated side-by-side. The figure contains approximately **8–10 individual borehole lithological columns** arranged across the page and connected by **correlation lines** (tie lines) linking equivalent stratigraphic horizons between wells. This type of figure is also called a **геолого-литологический разрез по скважинам** (geological-lithological cross-section through boreholes).

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers multiple boreholes (скважины) drilled during the regional survey of northern Kazakhstan (map sheets **M-42 and N-42**, 1:200,000 scale). Based on the visible column headers and annotations, the boreholes referenced appear to include references to:

- **Скважины (boreholes)** with designations partially legible, likely numbered in sequence (e.g., скв. №1, №2, №3, etc.)
- Several column headers contain text referencing **"Стратиграфический разрез по..."** (Stratigraphic section through...) followed by borehole identifiers
- References to **"Таблица №"** (Table No.) annotations appear on several columns, linking them to tabular data elsewhere in the report
- One header references **"обнажению берег реки"** or similar — suggesting at least one section is from a **natural outcrop** (natural river bank exposure) rather than a drillhole
- Depth ranges appear to extend from surface to approximately **1,000–1,500+ metres** based on the vertical scale of the longest columns (leftmost and rightmost columns are the longest)
- Stratigraphic notations visible on the column margins include **Cyrillic index labels** such as: **C₁, C₂** (Carboniferous), **D** (Devonian), **P** (Permian), and possibly **T** (Triassic) or **J** (Jurassic) subdivisions — consistent with Paleozoic basement geology typical of northern Kazakhstan

---

## Key Content

### Column Layout
- The columns are arranged roughly **left to right across the page**, with the two longest columns at the far left and far right, suggesting these represent the **deepest or most complete reference boreholes**
- Shorter columns in the center represent shallower or partial sections
- **Correlation tie lines** (straight diagonal lines) connect equivalent beds between columns, showing stratigraphic continuity or unconformities across the survey area

### Lithological Symbols (standard Soviet notation visible)
The following lithological patterns are distinguishable within the columns:
- **Horizontal line hatching** — shales or mudstones (аргиллиты/глинистые сланцы)
- **Brick/block pattern** — limestones or carbonates (известняки)
- **Dotted/stippled pattern** — sandstones or coarse clastic rocks (песчаники)
- **Diagonal cross-hatching** — possibly intrusive rocks or altered zones (изменённые породы)
- **Wavy/irregular pattern** — possibly tuffs, volcanics, or mixed lithologies
- **Dotted coarse pattern (large dots)** — conglomerates or coarse sandstones (конгломераты/гравелиты)
- Some columns show **thin regular lamination patterns** suggesting rhythmic alternation (flysch-type sequences or cyclic sedimentation)

### Stratigraphic Labels on Column Margins
Stratigraphic index labels are written to the right or left of each column in standard Soviet notation:
- Labels such as **C₁^v, C₁^t, C₂, D₃, P₁** etc. appear repeatedly
- These suggest the sections penetrate **Lower to Upper Carboniferous** and possibly **Upper Devonian** sequences — consistent with the Paleozoic platform cover and basement of northern Kazakhstan

### Correlation Lines
- Multiple sets of **diagonal tie lines** cross between columns, some connecting 3–4 columns simultaneously
- These lines appear to track **key marker horizons** (maркирующие горизонты) — likely distinctive limestone beds, unconformities, or coal seams
- Some correlation lines appear nearly horizontal (suggesting consistent depth), while others are strongly diagonal (suggesting structural dip or facies change)

### Depth Scale
- A **graphic depth scale** is visible at the bottom of the figure (noted as "Масштаб (вертикальный) на метры" or similar)
- The scale appears to be approximately **1:2,000 or 1:5,000 vertical**, typical for Soviet composite borehole sections of this era

The following text elements are transcribed as accurately as possible from the visible annotations (noting that image resolution limits full transcription):

- *"Разрез № 2 (в обнажении)"* — Section No. 2 (in outcrop)
